name: Deep-Dish Chicken Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 5-quart Dutch oven or saucepot over high heat, bring chickens, pepper, marjoram, water, and salt to boiling. Reduce heat to low; cover and simmer until chicken is fork-tender (about 35 minutes).
2. Remove chicken with a slotted spoon and discard bones and skin. Cut meat into 1-inch pieces.
3. In a 3-quart saucepan over high heat, bring carrots, celery, onions, and reserved broth to boiling. Reduce heat to low; cover and simmer until vegetables are almost tender (about 10 minutes). Remove from heat and transfer vegetables to a medium-sized bowl with a slotted spoon.
4. Blend half cup flour with 3/4 cup half-and-half in a small bowl. Gradually stir into the saucepan mixture, stirring in remaining half-and-half. Cook over low heat until sauce thickens slightly. Stir in chicken, cooked vegetables, thawed lima beans, mushrooms, and 1-1/2 teaspoons salt. Pour mixture into a 13x9-inch baking dish.
5. Prepare pastry: In a medium bowl, combine 3 cups flour and 1-1/2 teaspoons salt. Add shortening with a pastry blender or two knives; sprinkle about 1/2 cup water, a tablespoon at a time, mixing lightly until dough is moist enough to hold together. Form into a ball.
6. On a lightly floured surface, roll out two-thirds of the dough into a 15x11-inch rectangle. Cut several small slits in the center of the pastry. Place pastry over chicken mixture in the baking dish, trimming excess and folding under gently all around. Roll remaining dough to make decorative design on top.
7. Beat egg yolk with 1 teaspoon water in a cup. Brush pastry with egg mixture. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F for 1 hour or until crust is golden brown and chicken mixture is heated through.
